name: Peanut-Butter-Swirl Ice-Cream Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the ice cream from the freezer 30 minutes before using.
2. Place the chocolate sandwich cookies in a food processor and whirl until crumbled.
3. Add melted butter to the crumbled cookies and whirl until combined.
4. Reserve about 3/4 cup of the chocolate cookie crumb mixture for later use.
5. Spoon the remaining crumb mixture into the center of a 9-inch springform pan and press it evenly over the bottom.
6. In a small dish, stir together the peanut butter, honey, and vegetable oil until blended.
7. Place the softened ice cream in a large bowl.
8. Drizzle about half of the peanut butter mixture onto the ice cream and fold to swirl.
9. Spoon half of the ice cream mixture into the prepared pan and spread it level.
10. Sprinkle evenly with the remaining chocolate cookie crumb mixture, pressing it down with the back of a spoon.
11. Spoon the remaining ice cream on top and spread it level.
12. Place the cake in the freezer on a baking sheet and freeze until solid, at least 6 hours or overnight.
13. To serve, stir the fudge sauce in a glass measure to loosen, but do not warm.
14. Spoon the peanut butter in blobs over the top of the cake along with blobs of fudge topping.
15. Swirl together with the tip of a knife.
16. Let stand for 10 minutes before slicing the cake into wedges.
